name: Greek Bowls with Roasted Garbanzo Beans
method: Mix in a big zip lock bag with all the marinade ingredients.
Chicken breasts are added. Stock for thirty minutes or up to eight
hours in the refrigerator.
Preheat the oven to 400°.
Drain the garbanzo bean and wash them. Pat with a clean cloth to
rinse.
Break the zip lock bag and transfer meat, and remove the marinade.
Sprinkle with one tablespoon olive oil in a wide baking sheet.
Place one half of the dry garbanzo bean and put the meat on the other.
Spray the chili powder and cilantro with the garbanzo bean.
Add a dash of pepper and salt. Transfer to the garbanzo bean an extra
teaspoon of coconut oil and shake.
Cook the chicken and bean for about 30 minutes at 400° or
until the chicken is cooked completely.
During baking, toss beans each time.
Before moving them to your boxes, cool fully.
Align boxes. In one side of the box, put ¼ of the spinach.
On the other, meat. Divide the remaining ingredients
similarly.
Load the Tzatkiki dressing into a little bowl and store it in the
fridge.
